message:
test(raptorq): bounds-check assertions, runtime fallback boundary, and supported-profile provenance helper

Three RaptorQ correctness/safety improvements:

linalg.rs — GaussianSolver::set_coefficient() and set_rhs() previously
indexed into self.matrix and self.rhs without verifying that the row/col
arguments were within the declared dimensions, leading to confusing
panics deep inside Vec indexing on out-of-bounds calls. Added explicit
assert! statements with descriptive messages ("row out of bounds",
"column out of bounds") so misuse fails immediately at the API boundary.
Three new should_panic tests verify the assertions fire for out-of-range
row, out-of-range column on set_coefficient, and out-of-range row on
set_rhs.

decision_contract.rs — Two new tests pin down the live runtime fallback
boundary against the canonical telemetry contract:
- runtime_fallback_reason_boundary_matches_live_contract verifies the
  exact ordered list G7_RUNTIME_FALLBACK_REASONS is the four canonical
  reasons (PolicyBudgetExhausted, UnknownLowConfidence,
  RegressionLowConfidence, Unclassified) and that each is recognized by
  is_runtime_fallback_reason().
- broader_contract_only_triggers_are_not_runtime_emittable_reasons
  verifies that broader-contract triggers like "decode_mismatch_detected",
  "proof_replay_mismatch", "none", and "unknown_reason" are NOT
  runtime-emittable, preventing accidental scope creep.

gf256.rs — New shared test helper:
assert_supported_profile_request_scrubs_provenance_while_preserving_profile_truth(),
which encodes the invariant that env-requesting a supported profile
pack (matching the host arch) preserves profile truth (correct
profile_pack id, architecture_class, no fallback_reason) while still
scrubbing canonical-selection provenance to manual-override constants.
This consolidates duplicated assertion blocks across multiple existing
tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
